title: Message
description: A Slack message object representing a single message in a conversation. Messages can contain text, blocks, attachments, files, and reactions.

subtype.description: Optional subtype indicating a specialized message type such as bot_message, channel_join, channel_leave, etc.

channel.description: The channel ID where the message was posted.

user.description: The user ID of the message author.

text.description: The text content of the message.

ts.description: Unique timestamp identifier for the message, also serves as the message ID. Format is Unix epoch with microseconds (e.g., "1503435956.000247").

thread_ts.description: The timestamp of the parent message in a thread. Present only for threaded replies.

reply_count.description: Number of replies in the thread.

reply_users_count.description: Number of unique users who have replied in the thread.

latest_reply.description: Timestamp of the most recent reply in the thread.

reply_users.description: Array of user IDs who have replied in the thread.

blocks.description: An array of Block Kit layout blocks used for rich message formatting.

files.description: An array of file objects attached to the message.

reactions.description: An array of emoji reactions on this message.

edited.description: Edit information if the message was edited.

bot_id.description: Bot ID if the message was posted by a bot.

bot_profile.description: Bot profile information if posted by a bot.

metadata.description: Custom metadata attached to the message by an application.
